summary: DHH argues that Progressive Web Applications have finally become a viable alternative to native mobile apps for B2B startups in 2024. He traces the long struggle of startups needing to build for web, iOS, and Android simultaneously, which forces them to raise capital and hire large teams. Apple's recent addition of web push notifications and improved PWA support in Safari has removed the last major blocker. He recommends B2B startups focus on web-first development and only pursue native apps after achieving product-market fit, noting that 37signals is going all-in on PWAs for their new ONCE products.
key_points: B2B startups face an unfair burden building for web, iOS, and Android simultaneously, forcing them into external funding and large teams
Apple's addition of web push notifications and badge counting in Safari finally removes the last major blocker for PWAs as a native app alternative
Apple likely delayed PWA support for years to protect App Store revenue, only relenting under pressure from the EU's Digital Markets Act
B2B startups should target web-first and only invest in native apps after achieving product-market fit and profitability
37signals is going all-in on PWAs for their ONCE products, pairing web-based delivery with customer self-hosting
money_quotes: We built Basecamp back in 2003 because we could do it ourselves. A single developer and a couple of designers was all it took.
Everything just takes longer on mobile because the materials are harder to work with than the web.
You don't have to be much of a conspiracy theorist to ponder why it took Apple almost a decade to bring web push notifications to the iPhone.

Apple makes billions of dollars in toll fees from charging app makers 30% of their revenue to live in the App Store, and gating push notifications behind that toll has been an incredibly effective tool to enforce that distribution monopoly.
The hallowed promise of web applications being viable and competitive alternatives to native efforts is finally here.
I'm now going to recommend that every B2B software startup begins by targeting the web first.
If technical founder teams can't do it themselves, they have to hire, and if they have to hire, they have to raise capital. I hate that.
key_insight: Apple's belated embrace of web push notifications has finally made PWAs a realistic alternative to native mobile apps, freeing B2B startups from the capital-intensive burden of building for three platforms simultaneously.
